description_long: This object is an irregularly shaped stone block with a rough, natural surface and a light tan to reddish coloration. One of its broadest faces features a series of parallel, shallow incised lines running across its length, suggesting either tool marks or intentional scoring. The block's edges are jagged and uneven, with some chipped and fractured areas, giving it a weathered appearance. The underside of the block displays a hand-written marking or catalog number in black ink, indicating it may have been part of an archaeological or construction inventory. The stone's texture is visibly coarse, with granular inclusions and a matte finish. Its overall form is wedge-like, tapering slightly from one end to the other.
description: A rough, tan-reddish stone block with incised parallel lines on one face and jagged, chipped edges. The surface is coarse and matte, with visible weathering and a hand-written marking on the underside.
description_view_0: The broadest face is visible, showing parallel incised lines and the block's wedge-like shape.
description_view_1: A narrow side view reveals the block's thickness and the rough, chipped texture along the edge.
description_view_2: The underside is shown, featuring a hand-written marking and a more granular, uneven surface.
description_view_3: Another narrow side view, highlighting the irregular, fractured edge and the stone's layered appearance.
attribute_location_context: archaeological site or historic urban retaining wall
attribute_weathering_pattern: Chipped corners and rough, granular surface indicate exposure to the elements and mechanical wear. No significant moss or water smoothing is visible.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: Parallel incised lines on one face and a hand-written catalog number on the underside.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: Likely to be stacked or placed adjacent to other similar blocks, forming part of a larger structure or barrier.
